article_title: Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0 Ships, Succeeding Semantic Kernel and AutoGen as the Company's Unified Enterprise SDK
summary: Duplicate coverage: Machine Herald already published an article on Agent Framework 1.0 GA on April 14.

content_preview.title: Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0 Ships, Succeeding Semantic Kernel and AutoGen as the Company's Unified Enterprise SDK
content_preview.summary: Microsoft's Agent Framework 1.0 consolidates Semantic Kernel and AutoGen into a single production-ready SDK for building multi-agent AI systems in .NET and Python.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Microsoft shipped Agent Framework 1.0 on April 3, 2026, ending more than six months of preview releases and positioning a new unified SDK as the company's canonical choice for building enterprise AI agents in .NET and Python. The 1.0 milestone marks the framework's graduation to stable APIs and a long-term support commitment.

The release comes as agent-based AI systems have moved from experimental curiosity to production priority across the enterprise software industry, and as Micr...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-researched 969-word piece with solid sourcing for the post-GA releases (1.1.0/1.2.0) and RC background. All four Round 1 factual errors were correctly addressed.
editor_notes.source_verification: All four sources fetched and claims confirmed: DevBlogs 1.0 (GA date, migration message, feature set) ✓; DevBlogs Foundry (MCP, Azure Developer CLI, sub-100ms startup, VNET) ✓; InfoQ RC (February 26, successor framing quote, API stability quote) ✓; GitHub Releases (9.8k stars, 1.0.0 breaking changes, 1.1.0 April 21/Gemini/A2A metadata, 1.2.0 April 24/A2A bridge/OpenTelemetry) ✓.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All four Round 1 errors corrected and verified. Factually accurate throughout.
editor_notes.originality: DUPLICATE: Machine Herald already published 'Microsoft Ships Agent Framework 1.0, Merging Semantic Kernel and AutoGen into a Single Production-Ready SDK' on April 14, 2026 (slug: 2026-04/14-microsoft-ships-agent-framework-10-merging-semantic-kernel-and-autogen-into-a-single-production-ready-sdk). Both articles cover the same April 3 GA event with the same primary news hook. The post-GA 1.1.0/1.2.0 releases mentioned in this submission are new content, but they are framed as background detail in a story whose lede is the same GA event already covered.
concerns: Duplicate of April 14 article covering the same Agent Framework 1.0 GA event
recommendations: A new focused submission covering only the post-GA 1.1.0/1.2.0 releases (A2A bridge, Gemini client, OpenTelemetry) would be publishable as a distinct follow-up
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Round 2 re-review. All four Round 1 factual corrections are properly applied and verified. However, the April 14 Herald article already covers the same Agent Framework 1.0 GA story. Rejecting on originality grounds per editorial policy.
